---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: df8a04e38062c487dce36ffdba2041d9a90e2f91
findings:
  - { id: judged-detail-skips-authoritative-source-filter, kind: judged, text: "unmetACDetail (cmd/verdi/closepreflight.go:209,228,248-254) computes per-kind 'satisfied' over raw evidence.LoadRecords output, which includes BOTH source:ci and source:local records ('Fold decides which to trust via its Preview flag', records.go:59-60). The closure gate's verdict, by contrast, folds with Preview=false, whose filterCandidates (internal/evidence/candidates.go:19-27) drops every source:local record before kindStatus runs. So the which-AC-is-unmet set comes from ci-only semantics (correct, per dc-2) but the which-kind-is-missing rendering uses ci+local semantics — a second, differently-derived per-kind status, exactly the drift class dc-2 and ac-1's attestation clause ('no second eligibility computation exists anywhere in the diff') exist to prevent. Concrete failure: a story whose only passing behavioral record is source:local (a local run synced into the derived tree) fails eligibility at the gate, the AC lands in unmet, but unmetACDetail sees the local pass, marks the kind satisfied, and prints no missing-kind/path line for it — violating ac-1's 'Each unmet AC names its missing evidence kind and the exact on-disk path' (the disclosure silently vanishes for precisely the artifact the fold is refusing on). Current() over the mixed set can likewise elect a later source:local record as the current one for a (kind,producer) group, shadowing the ci record the fold actually judged. No fixture in closepreflight_test.go seeds a source:local record, so ac-3's agreement suite never exercises this divergence. (confidence 0.80)" }
  - { id: judged-feature-detail-uses-story-and-semantics, kind: judged, text: "For feature scope, runFeaturePreflightGate feeds unmetACDetail (closepreflightfeature.go:77), which checks every declared evidence kind independently and prints a remedy for each unsatisfied one — the story fold's AND-across-declared-kinds semantics. But the feature fold's outcome floor is an OR across an AC's signals (foldFeatureAC, featurefold.go:196-204: attested OR any current passing record clears the floor) — a fact the implementation's own test comment concedes (closepreflightfeature_test.go:138-143: 'unlike the story-level fold's AND-across-declared-kinds'). Concrete failure: a feature AC declaring [behavioral, attestation] with a passing behavioral record (floor satisfied) that is unmet only because an implementing story is still open (status pending) — the preflight prints 'ac-N attestation: no file at .verdi/attestations/\u003cfeature\u003e/ac-N.md; scaffold it with `verdi attest`', instructing the operator to author an attestation the feature gate does not require and whose authoring cannot change the verdict. That is a false remedy from a re-derived requirement notion, contrary to ac-1's intent that each disclosure name the evidence actually missing for the fold and to dc-6's own bar that a wrong disclosure is 'exactly the kind of defect this story exists to eliminate'. The defect-class tests never construct this shape (floor satisfied via one kind + story open), so the agreement suite doesn't catch it. (confidence 0.55)" }
  - { id: judged-violated-kind-rendered-as-missing, kind: judged, text: "For a violated AC, unmetACDetail renders the failing kind as '\u003cac\u003e \u003ckind\u003e: no current passing record; derived-tree root probed: ...' (closepreflight.go:259) — the identical text used for no-signal/pending — and TestRunPreflight_StoryScope_DefectClasses' violated subtest pins exactly that (closepreflight_test.go:293-295). ac-3 names 'an unmet AC per evidence kind (no signal, pending, violated)' as distinct defect classes, and the spec's outcome section frames the enrichment as rendering 'an AC's own evidenced/pending/violated/no-signal status'. A violated kind is not missing evidence — a current failing record exists in the derived tree — so the remedy the line implies (produce a record at the probed root) misdescribes the actual blocker (a failing witness that must be fixed or superseded), even though the literal text ('no current passing record') is technically true. Mild semantic flattening of the fold's three unsatisfied statuses into one disclosure shape; the fold's already-computed per-AC Summary (e.g. 'static:fail') carries the distinction and is discarded. (confidence 0.30)" }
digest: sha256:eb62dce3fe9f7f37a92636b2ad6b7175b6b562cdeb31bee4f4f4bbb9e1ccce6c
integrity: sha256:1418b4316e2c7f4d2a9b00fa4d66396a7161ecff16eb0cbe5851c978b3953b49
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"detail-skips-authoritative-source-filter\",\"text\":\"unmetACDetail (cmd/verdi/closepreflight.go:209,228,248-254) computes per-kind 'satisfied' over raw evidence.LoadRecords output, which includes BOTH source:ci and source:local records ('Fold decides which to trust via its Preview flag', records.go:59-60). The closure gate's verdict, by contrast, folds with Preview=false, whose filterCandidates (internal/evidence/candidates.go:19-27) drops every source:local record before kindStatus runs. So the which-AC-is-unmet set comes from ci-only semantics (correct, per dc-2) but the which-kind-is-missing rendering uses ci+local semantics — a second, differently-derived per-kind status, exactly the drift class dc-2 and ac-1's attestation clause ('no second eligibility computation exists anywhere in the diff') exist to prevent. Concrete failure: a story whose only passing behavioral record is source:local (a local run synced into the derived tree) fails eligibility at the gate, the AC lands in unmet, but unmetACDetail sees the local pass, marks the kind satisfied, and prints no missing-kind/path line for it — violating ac-1's 'Each unmet AC names its missing evidence kind and the exact on-disk path' (the disclosure silently vanishes for precisely the artifact the fold is refusing on). Current() over the mixed set can likewise elect a later source:local record as the current one for a (kind,producer) group, shadowing the ci record the fold actually judged. No fixture in closepreflight_test.go seeds a source:local record, so ac-3's agreement suite never exercises this divergence.\",\"confidence\":0.8},{\"id\":\"feature-detail-uses-story-and-semantics\",\"text\":\"For feature scope, runFeaturePreflightGate feeds unmetACDetail (closepreflightfeature.go:77), which checks every declared evidence kind independently and prints a remedy for each unsatisfied one — the story fold's AND-across-declared-kinds semantics. But the feature fold's outcome floor is an OR across an AC's signals (foldFeatureAC, featurefold.go:196-204: attested OR any current passing record clears the floor) — a fact the implementation's own test comment concedes (closepreflightfeature_test.go:138-143: 'unlike the story-level fold's AND-across-declared-kinds'). Concrete failure: a feature AC declaring [behavioral, attestation] with a passing behavioral record (floor satisfied) that is unmet only because an implementing story is still open (status pending) — the preflight prints 'ac-N attestation: no file at .verdi/attestations/\u003cfeature\u003e/ac-N.md; scaffold it with `verdi attest`', instructing the operator to author an attestation the feature gate does not require and whose authoring cannot change the verdict. That is a false remedy from a re-derived requirement notion, contrary to ac-1's intent that each disclosure name the evidence actually missing for the fold and to dc-6's own bar that a wrong disclosure is 'exactly the kind of defect this story exists to eliminate'. The defect-class tests never construct this shape (floor satisfied via one kind + story open), so the agreement suite doesn't catch it.\",\"confidence\":0.55},{\"id\":\"violated-kind-rendered-as-missing\",\"text\":\"For a violated AC, unmetACDetail renders the failing kind as '\u003cac\u003e \u003ckind\u003e: no current passing record; derived-tree root probed: ...' (closepreflight.go:259) — the identical text used for no-signal/pending — and TestRunPreflight_StoryScope_DefectClasses' violated subtest pins exactly that (closepreflight_test.go:293-295). ac-3 names 'an unmet AC per evidence kind (no signal, pending, violated)' as distinct defect classes, and the spec's outcome section frames the enrichment as rendering 'an AC's own evidenced/pending/violated/no-signal status'. A violated kind is not missing evidence — a current failing record exists in the derived tree — so the remedy the line implies (produce a record at the probed root) misdescribes the actual blocker (a failing witness that must be fixed or superseded), even though the literal text ('no current passing record') is technically true. Mild semantic flattening of the fold's three unsatisfied statuses into one disclosure shape; the fold's already-computed per-AC Summary (e.g. 'static:fail') carries the distinction and is discarded.\",\"confidence\":0.3}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/close-preflight@df8a04e38062c487dce36ffdba2041d9a90e2f91, spec/close-preflight@48d98b3a39de475e40b37255bc7ce257ccc21333], digest: sha256:eb62dce3fe9f7f37a92636b2ad6b7175b6b562cdeb31bee4f4f4bbb9e1ccce6c, integrity: sha256:1418b4316e2c7f4d2a9b00fa4d66396a7161ecff16eb0cbe5851c978b3953b49 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-detail-skips-authoritative-source-filter** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: unmetACDetail (cmd/verdi/closepreflight.go:209,228,248-254) computes per-kind 'satisfied' over raw evidence.LoadRecords output, which includes BOTH source:ci and source:local records ('Fold decides which to trust via its Preview flag', records.go:59-60). The closure gate's verdict, by contrast, folds with Preview=false, whose filterCandidates (internal/evidence/candidates.go:19-27) drops every source:local record before kindStatus runs. So the which-AC-is-unmet set comes from ci-only semantics (correct, per dc-2) but the which-kind-is-missing rendering uses ci+local semantics — a second, differently-derived per-kind status, exactly the drift class dc-2 and ac-1's attestation clause ('no second eligibility computation exists anywhere in the diff') exist to prevent. Concrete failure: a story whose only passing behavioral record is source:local (a local run synced into the derived tree) fails eligibility at the gate, the AC lands in unmet, but unmetACDetail sees the local pass, marks the kind satisfied, and prints no missing-kind/path line for it — violating ac-1's 'Each unmet AC names its missing evidence kind and the exact on-disk path' (the disclosure silently vanishes for precisely the artifact the fold is refusing on). Current() over the mixed set can likewise elect a later source:local record as the current one for a (kind,producer) group, shadowing the ci record the fold actually judged. No fixture in closepreflight_test.go seeds a source:local record, so ac-3's agreement suite never exercises this divergence. (confidence 0.80)
- **judged-feature-detail-uses-story-and-semantics** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: For feature scope, runFeaturePreflightGate feeds unmetACDetail (closepreflightfeature.go:77), which checks every declared evidence kind independently and prints a remedy for each unsatisfied one — the story fold's AND-across-declared-kinds semantics. But the feature fold's outcome floor is an OR across an AC's signals (foldFeatureAC, featurefold.go:196-204: attested OR any current passing record clears the floor) — a fact the implementation's own test comment concedes (closepreflightfeature_test.go:138-143: 'unlike the story-level fold's AND-across-declared-kinds'). Concrete failure: a feature AC declaring [behavioral, attestation] with a passing behavioral record (floor satisfied) that is unmet only because an implementing story is still open (status pending) — the preflight prints 'ac-N attestation: no file at .verdi/attestations/<feature>/ac-N.md; scaffold it with `verdi attest`', instructing the operator to author an attestation the feature gate does not require and whose authoring cannot change the verdict. That is a false remedy from a re-derived requirement notion, contrary to ac-1's intent that each disclosure name the evidence actually missing for the fold and to dc-6's own bar that a wrong disclosure is 'exactly the kind of defect this story exists to eliminate'. The defect-class tests never construct this shape (floor satisfied via one kind + story open), so the agreement suite doesn't catch it. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-violated-kind-rendered-as-missing** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: For a violated AC, unmetACDetail renders the failing kind as '<ac> <kind>: no current passing record; derived-tree root probed: ...' (closepreflight.go:259) — the identical text used for no-signal/pending — and TestRunPreflight_StoryScope_DefectClasses' violated subtest pins exactly that (closepreflight_test.go:293-295). ac-3 names 'an unmet AC per evidence kind (no signal, pending, violated)' as distinct defect classes, and the spec's outcome section frames the enrichment as rendering 'an AC's own evidenced/pending/violated/no-signal status'. A violated kind is not missing evidence — a current failing record exists in the derived tree — so the remedy the line implies (produce a record at the probed root) misdescribes the actual blocker (a failing witness that must be fixed or superseded), even though the literal text ('no current passing record') is technically true. Mild semantic flattening of the fold's three unsatisfied statuses into one disclosure shape; the fold's already-computed per-AC Summary (e.g. 'static:fail') carries the distinction and is discarded. (confidence 0.30)
